summary: Split the former monolithic fighter file `src/melee/ft/ft_0C88.c` into many smaller common-fighter action files, updated build splits and object configuration, moved declarations out of the catch-all `ft_0C88.h`, and replaced many opaque or old entry-point names with action-specific headers and symbols. The split covered common states such as DownSpot, Barrel, Walk, Turn, TurnRun, Dash, Run, RunDirect, RunBrake, Jump, KneeBend, JumpAerial, Fall, and FallAerial. Most new objects were marked Matching in `configure.py`; `ftCo_Barrel.c` remained NonMatching. Review evidence is sparse: the only excerpted human comment says “Nice work!”.
what_changed: Deleted the large contents of `src/melee/ft/ft_0C88.c` and redistributed its code into action-specific common fighter source files under `src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/` plus a new `src/melee/ft/ft_0C8C.c` segment.

Shrank `src/melee/ft/ft_0C88.h` substantially, from the changed-file summary showing 107 deleted lines and 2 added lines, and introduced per-action headers such as `ftCo_JumpAerial.h`, `ftCo_Fall.h`, `ftCo_Jump.h`, `ftCo_Turn.h`, `ftCo_Run.h`, `ftCo_Barrel.h`, and others.

Updated `config/GALE01/splits.txt` so the old `melee/ft/ft_0C88.c` text range `0x800C884C` to `0x800CCE94` became many consecutive object ranges, including `ftCo_DownSpot.c`, `ft_0C8C.c`, `ftCo_Barrel.c`, `ftCo_Walk.c`, `ftCo_Turn.c`, `ftCo_TurnRun.c`, `ftCo_Dash.c`, `ftCo_Run.c`, `ftCo_RunDirect.c`, `ftCo_RunBrake.c`, `ftCo_Jump.c`, `ftCo_KneeBend.c`, `ftCo_JumpAerial.c`, `ftCo_Fall.c`, and `ftCo_FallAerial.c`.

Updated `configure.py` to replace one `Object(NonMatching, "melee/ft/ft_0C88.c")` entry with many object entries. Most split files were marked `Matching`; `melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_Barrel.c` was explicitly left `NonMatching`.

Renamed several symbols in `config/GALE01/symbols.txt`, including `ftCo_SmashTurn_Enter` to `ftCo_Turn_Enter_Smash`, `fn_800CB250` to `ftCo_Jump_Enter`, and `ftCo_800CC730`/`ftCo_800CC830`/`ftCo_800CC8DC` to `ftCo_Fall_Enter`, `ftCo_Fall_Enter_YoshiEgg`, and `ftCo_Fall_Enter_YoshiEgg_Kirby`.

Updated many fighter and common-action users to include narrow headers instead of the broad `ft/ft_0C88.h`; for example Captain Falcon special files now include `ftCommon/ftCo_Fall.h` and call `ftCo_Fall_Enter(gobj)` instead of `ftCo_800CC730(gobj)`.

Added a new `ftCo_Barrel.c`/`.h` pair containing barrel-state callbacks and entry points such as `ftCo_Barrel_Enter`, `ftCo_Barrel_Anim`, `ftCo_Barrel_IASA`, `ftCo_Barrel_Phys`, `ftCo_Barrel_Coll`, `ftCo_Barrel_Accessory1_Cb`, and `ftCo_800C92E4`.
smart_moves: The split preserved the original address order in `splits.txt`, making the object-boundary change auditable and keeping `.text`, `.sdata`, `.sdata2`, and `.rodata` ranges attached to their new files.
The PR paired source-file splitting with build-system changes in `configure.py`, so matching status was explicit per new object rather than hidden behind the old monolithic `ft_0C88.c`.
Replacing broad `ft_0C88.h` includes with action-specific headers reduced dependency sprawl and made call sites reveal the actual common action they depend on.
Renaming formerly address-like functions to semantic common-action names improved future searchability and reduced repeated use of unknown symbols, especially for fall and jump transitions.
Leaving `ftCo_Barrel.c` as `NonMatching` while marking the other split pieces `Matching` avoided overstating matching progress and isolated the remaining problematic action file.
decomp_lessons: When splitting a large decompilation file, update `splits.txt`, `configure.py`, symbols, headers, and downstream includes in the same PR so object boundaries, build behavior, and source dependencies remain synchronized.
Use the original address ranges as the source of truth for contiguous file extraction; this PR demonstrates a clean address-ordered split from `0x800C884C` through `0x800CCE94`.
Semantic action headers such as `ftCo_Fall.h`, `ftCo_Jump.h`, and `ftCo_Turn.h` make call sites easier to reason about than a catch-all address-range header.
It is acceptable and useful to split a file even when one extracted object remains NonMatching, as long as the nonmatching object is isolated and clearly marked.
Symbol renames should be propagated to call sites immediately; replacing `ftCo_800CC730` with `ftCo_Fall_Enter` makes future fighter-special cleanup easier.
Evidence for reviewer intent is weak in this slice; aside from a “Nice work!” comment, there is no detailed review discussion or review-comment guidance available.
naming_conventions: Common fighter action files use `ftCo_<Action>.c` and `ftCo_<Action>.h`, for example `ftCo_JumpAerial.c`, `ftCo_RunBrake.c`, and `ftCo_FallAerial.c`.
Common action entry functions use `ftCo_<Action>_Enter`, with variants appended after the action, as shown by `ftCo_Fall_Enter`, `ftCo_Fall_Enter_YoshiEgg`, and `ftCo_Fall_Enter_YoshiEgg_Kirby`.
Action callbacks use suffixes such as `_Anim`, `_IASA`, `_Phys`, `_Coll`, `_Accessory1_Cb`, `_Take_Dmg_Cb`, and `_Death2_Cb`, as shown in `ftCo_Barrel.c`.
More specific name order was preferred for smash turn: `ftCo_SmashTurn_Enter` became `ftCo_Turn_Enter_Smash`, grouping it under the Turn action.
Headers retain address-based include guards in the shown new `ftCo_Barrel.h`, e.g. `GALE01_0C9058`, while exported prototypes include address comments such as `/* 0C9078 */`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Converted a monolithic NonMatching object into many split objects, allowing most sections to be marked `Matching` independently in `configure.py`.
Preserved contiguous text section boundaries in `splits.txt`, with each extracted source file assigned exact start/end addresses and any associated small-data ranges.
Kept `ftCo_Barrel.c` as `NonMatching`, suggesting the split separated already-matching code from one remaining difficult region rather than forcing all extracted files to match.
Used symbol-table renames to replace anonymous or address-based functions with semantic names while retaining addresses and sizes in `config/GALE01/symbols.txt`.
The new `ftCo_Barrel.c` still includes `<placeholder.h>`, indicating some types or declarations remained provisional in that nonmatching portion.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.interpretation: Positive but non-specific review feedback; no actionable technical review details are present in the provided slice.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that make `ftCo_Barrel.c` Matching.
Search for remaining includes of `ft/ft_0C88.h` after PR #1666.
Search for remaining calls to `ftCo_800CC730`, `ftCo_800CC830`, and `ftCo_800CC8DC` after the fall-entry rename.
Compare subsequent naming of common fighter action entry functions against the `ftCo_<Action>_Enter` pattern used here.
Inspect whether `ft_0C8C.c` was later renamed to a semantic common-action file.
